_doc: Backfill sources for EXISTING entries. Auto-bootstrapped + manually agent-sourced.
_phase6_status: COMPLETE — all 4 agents (p6a 17, p6b 56, p6c 42, p6d 16, p6e 21) + company-match bootstrap (69) finished. ~14 needs-source flags + several name/company corrections noted in audit.md.

sw1.notes: 7B-param open-source VLA trained on 970K Open X-Embodiment demonstrations.

sources.title: Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models (arXiv)

sw2.notes: RT-X built by Open X-Embodiment Collaboration with Google DeepMind, 21 institutions, 22 robots.

sw3.notes: GPU-accelerated open-source framework for robot learning, successor to Isaac Gym/Orbit.

sources.title: What Matters in Learning from Offline Human Demonstrations for Robot Manipulation (RoboMimic)

sw4.notes: Modular framework for robot learning from demonstration, Stanford SVL/ARISE Initiative, CoRL 2021 oral.

sources.title: Highly Dynamic Quadruped Locomotion via WBC and MPC

ca1.notes: MIT BIOMIMETICS Cheetah whole-body controller (WBC+MPC), up to 3.7 m/s.

sw6.notes: Acquired by DeepMind Oct 2021, open-sourced Apache 2.0 May 2022.

bm1.notes: UCSD Hao Su Lab; up to 30,000+ FPS.

sources.title: BMW Press: humanoid robot test at Spartanburg
Figure: F.02 contributed to 30,000+ vehicles at BMW

app1.notes: Smart Factory = humanoid manufacturing assembly (Figure 02 @ BMW, Apptronik Apollo @ Mercedes).

vc1.notes: Sequoia robotics thesis covering Skild, Collaborative Robotics, Robco.

a1.notes: UR10e 6-DOF cobot; 12.5 kg payload (upgraded 2021), 1300 mm reach, ±0.05 mm repeatability.

a2.notes: Franka Emika Panda 7-DOF research arm; predecessor to FR3.

e1.notes: 85 mm stroke, 5 kg payload, 20-235 N grip force.

e2.notes: E3M5R: 20 actuated DoF, 1 kHz EtherCAT, 4.3 kg.

j1.notes: FSA Actuator used inside Fourier's GRx humanoids; no dedicated FSA product page indexed.

j2.notes: Dynamixel-P PH42-020-S300-R: 5.1 Nm continuous, 340 g, RS-485.

c1.notes: Strain-wave reducer. Ratios 30:1-160:1, zero backlash, 1 arc-min accuracy.

c2.notes: KBM Series frameless brushless servo motors; sizes 10-118. Live fetch returned 403 (anti-bot block).

s1.notes: D455: 0.4-6 m range, 1280×720 depth @ 90 fps, 87°×58° FOV, 95 mm baseline.

s2.notes: Axia80: Fx/Fy ±500 N, Fz ±900 N, T ±20 Nm, EtherCAT 8 kHz, IP64.

d1.notes: HaptX Gloves G1: microfluidic smart-textile haptic gloves.

cp1.notes: AGX Orin: up to 275 TOPS, 256-bit LPDDR5, 15-60 W.

cp2.notes: Fanless AI IPC built on AGX Orin (32/64 GB).

f15.notes: Bootstrapped from company-match (LimX Dynamics (逐际动力)).

f6.notes: SPACED M1 likely refers to Muks Robotics SPACEO M1 — 167 cm wheeled social humanoid, ~$11k, launched March 2025.

sources.title: Humanoid robot Adam debuts at music festival in China

f12.notes: PNDbotics ADAM family: 1.6-1.67m, ~60-62kg, 25-44 DoF QDD actuators.

f13.notes: SPACED P1 likely refers to Muks Robotics SPACEO Pro/Prime (input name appears garbled).

f22.notes: MATRIX-1 by Matrix Robotics (Shanghai): 1.8m, 67kg, 77 DoF. Global debut at WAIC 2025.

f39.notes: KUAVO-S not a distinct SKU; likely refers to a KUAVO series variant. Company-level sourcing.

f44.notes: EngineAI PM01: 1.4m, ~42kg, 23 DoF, 300° waist rotation, 2 m/s+ speed.

f50.notes: EngineAI SA02: 1.25m, 25kg, 28 DoF, $5,300-5,500 companion humanoid announced WRC 2025.

sources.title: Kepler Robotics showcases K2 'Bumblebee' humanoid at ICRA 2025
Kepler Robotics Unveils K2 'Bumblebee' at IROS 2025

f54.notes: Kepler K2 Bumblebee: 175cm, 75kg, 52 DoF, 100 TOPS, $30K base.

f56.notes: Roban 2: Leju's compact/child-sized bipedal humanoid, launched ~Sept-Oct 2025.

sources.title: Humanoid 'Kuafu' debuts as torchbearer — People's Daily

f61.notes: KUAVO-MY general-purpose humanoid: ~1.7m, 26 DoF, 4.6 km/h. Torchbearer at China's 15th National Games (Nov 2025).

f67.notes: EngineAI T800: 1.85m, 85kg, 41 DoF, solid-state battery, ~$25K. Global debut WRC 2025.

f72.notes: Kepler K2 (Bumblebee) 5th-gen industrial humanoid. Mass production Sept 2025.

f88.notes: Generic 'KEPLER' entry — refers to Kepler Robotics humanoid line (K series).

f96.notes: MATRIX 4 designation not found by exact name in current lineup. Possible internal preview.

f106.notes: EngineAI SE01 (likely 'SEO1' input typo): full-size 170cm, ~55kg, 32 DoF flagship humanoid.

f123.notes: XTRON KUAVO-MY — Leju KUAVO-MY variant; XTRON appears to be a sub-brand.

en1.investigation: Verified as fabricated entry — no robot-battery vendor 'Ampere' exists.

a3.investigation: No verifiable product 'KINO X2'. Not Kinova X2. Possible hallucination.

a5.investigation: No verifiable manufacturer for 'PANDEL' robot arm. Likely hallucination.

a6.notes: 'PB-340' likely name-mangling of 'ultraArm P340'. Elephant Robotics 4-DOF compact arm.

a7.investigation: Pudu Robotics' official lineup does NOT include 'FlashBot Arm'. Pudu sells FlashBot (delivery robot) but no humanoid/arm SKU under that name.

a8.notes: Kinisi Robotics KR1 (Kinisi 01) general-purpose manipulator. Founded 2024; NYC + Bristol.

h1.notes: Chestnut Robotics: open-source $314 anthropomorphic hand, <400g, 7 motors, 16 joints.

h5.notes: RH50G2 not in current Inspire catalog; may be legacy designation.

sources.title: ZhaoWei releases new dexterous hand (Sina Finance)

h10.notes: Kyber Labs five-finger hand, 20 DoF, fully backdrivable.

h11.notes: AgiBot OmniHand Pro 2025: 12 active/19 total DoF, ≤750g, $14,610.

h12.notes: Wonik Robotics Allegro Hand V4: 4 fingers, 16 joints.

h18.investigation: No English or Chinese source for AfaRobot 远界机器人 AEP-H01 found.

h20.notes: RH50 SERIES not on current Inspire catalog (RH56-series listed). May be legacy.

h23.notes: CORRECTION: REVO 2 is by BrainCo, NOT 'AUTODISCOVERY/探索科技'. 383g, 6 active joints/11 DoF, 50N grip.

h28.notes: CORRECTION: ALLEX is by WIRobotics (Korea), NOT 'NXROBOTICS'. 20 joints, 15 active DoF, 30kg hook grip.

h30.notes: PaXini DexH5: 4-finger tactile hand, 9 DoF, 528+ tactile sensors.

h34.notes: SharpaWave: 22 active DoF, CES 2026 Innovation Award.

h35.notes: ETH ORCA Hand: 17 DoF, tendon-driven, <2000 CHF, MIT/CC licensed.

h36.notes: DexHand v2: ~19 DoF, Arduino Nano RP2040, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

h38.notes: X2: tendon-driven, autonomous L/R reconfiguration. Launched Feb 2026.

h40.notes: Robotera XHAND1: 12 active DoF, 80N grip, 24kg whole-hand lift.

h41.notes: PaXini DexH13: 4-finger, 16 DoF, 1140 tactile units + AI vision.

h42.notes: Mia Hand R&D: >520g, 3 actuators/4 DoF, 44-140N grasp.

h45.notes: ArtiXon: 20 DoF, 15 actuators, tendon-driven, foundation-model controlled.

h46.notes: DexcelRobotics Apex Hand: 21 DoF, flexible e-skin, >1000Hz refresh. Aug 2025.

h47.notes: CORRECTION: MagicHand S01 is by MagicLab, NOT 逐际动力/LimX. 11 DoF, 580g, 5kg max payload.

h48.notes: qb SoftHand2 Research: anthropomorphic, 19 joints, dual-synergy.

h52.notes: Mimic Robotics M1.0: ETH Zurich spinout. Universal dexterous hand + foundation manipulation model.

sources.title: TARS Demonstrates a Robot That Can Perform Hand Embroidery

f10.notes: TARS Robotics (Shanghai, founded Feb 2025); T-Series and A-Series platforms.

f19.investigation: Haier's actual humanoid is 'HIVA Haiwa' (Jul 2025, 165cm, 44 DoF). 'HAWO HANXI' not found.

f23.investigation: AgileX LIMO is a wheeled/tracked mobile education robot (Jetson Nano, 322x220x251mm, 4.2kg), NOT a humanoid. Categorizing under 整机平台 is incorrect.

f26.notes: EIR Technology (Sichuan, founded Sep 2024) SkyWalker 2 — 165cm, 55kg, 55 DoF, $85K.

f28.investigation: Ascento makes a two-wheeled jumping security robot ('Ascento Guard'), not a humanoid. 'RUFUS' not found.

f30.notes: MagicLab (Magic Atom, founded Dec 2023) MagicBot Gen1: 174cm, 70kg, 42 DoF.

f32.notes: Noetix Robotics (Songyan Power, Beijing, founded Sep 2023) DORA: 20-26 DoF.

f41.notes: X-Humanoid Tian Yi 2.0 — 35 DoF wheel-arm mobile manipulator.

f45.notes: Generative Bionics (Italy) GENE.01: full-body tactile skin. CES 2026 with AMD. $81M funding.

f48.notes: Tien Kung 2.0 by X-Humanoid; marathon world record Apr 2025.

f51.notes: Reachy 2 — CES 2025, dual 7-DoF arms. Pollen acquired by Hugging Face Apr 2025.

sources.title: World's most powerful humanoid aces backflip

f52.notes: PHYBOT M1 (Tsinghua spinoff): 172cm, 32 DoF, 530 N·m peak torque.

f53.notes: ROBROS (Korea) IGRIS-C — 154cm, 60kg, 31 DoF. Won bipedal walking at IEEE-RAS Humanoids 2024.

f55.notes: Borg Robotics (Detroit, founded 2022) Borg 01: modular bipedal/wheeled humanoid.

sources.title: Mentee Robotics NVIDIA Isaac case study

f59.notes: Mentee Robotics MenteeBot V3.0: 1.75m, acquired by Mobileye.

f60.notes: Lumos Robotics NIX — 80cm small humanoid for family companionship.

sources.title: 博实股份-哈工大合作研发的人形机器人

f62.notes: DexBot — industrial humanoid by Boshi Auto (博实股份) + Harbin Institute of Technology. 60 DoF.

f63.notes: Astribot S1: 165cm, 58kg, 36 DoF. NOTE: input says 'STAR1' but Astribot's product is 'S1'; 'STAR1' belongs to RobotEra. May be conflated.

f68.notes: Galaxea AI (星海图) R1 Pro: 26 DoF, dual 7-axis arms, 10kg/arm. RMB 199K starting.

sources.title: Alibaba leads $100M in X Square Robot

f74.notes: X Square Robot Quanta X2: 172cm, 62 DoF + WALL-A foundation model.

f75.notes: Westwood Robotics THEMIS V2 (UCLA RoMeLa spinoff): 1.6m, 40 DoF.

f76.notes: Hexagon Robotics AEON — launched Stockholm Jun 17 2025. NVIDIA/Microsoft/maxon partners.

f77.notes: Noetix Hobbs bionic robot head; Hobbs 3 = 32 DoF.

f80.notes: DOBOT Atom: 1.53m, 62kg, 41 DoF. Mar 2025 launch, RMB 199K start.

f85.notes: AeiROBOT (Korea, Hanyang U spinoff) ALICE 4: 160cm, 41 DoF, gearless linear actuators.

f87.notes: Siasun (新松) Songxing 松行 — bipedal humanoid in development.

f89.notes: Cartwheel Robotics Yogi: 76cm, 50+ DoF, soft silicone skin. Founder ex-Boston Dynamics.

f91.notes: RoboForce (Milpitas) TITAN industrial humanoid. Jensen Huang GTC 2025 keynote.

f93.notes: Elephant Robotics Mercury X1: 1.18m wheeled humanoid, 19 DoF. NOTE: 'ROBOTART X1' name not found; actual product is Mercury X1.

f97.notes: Topstar (拓斯达) Xiao Tuo: wheeled humanoid for injection-molding lines. Sep 12 2025.

f101.notes: Lanxin Robotics VersaBot VB-1: World's first pure vision-based humanoid (Aug 23 2024). NOTE: input lists 'VC-1' but actual is 'VB-1'.

f102.investigation: MagicLab lineup is MagicBot Gen1 + Z1; no 'MagicBot 2' found.

f103.notes: Clone Robotics Clone Alpha: 170cm, 206 artificial bones, Myofiber water-hydraulic muscles.

f107.notes: Boardwalk Robotics Alex (upper-torso humanoid, IHMC commercial partner). 19 DoF.

f108.notes: Humanoid (UK) HMND 01 Alpha. Same company as f58. Live data has broken image ref.

f109.notes: PaXini TORA-ONE: 1.46-1.86m, 21 DoF body / 47 with hands.

f112.notes: Same as f41 — X-Humanoid Tian Yi 2.0. f41 and f112 appear to be duplicates.

f113.notes: OWI RE/CO is a 106-piece STEM hobby kit (tracked off-road), NOT a humanoid platform. Recategorize.

f115.notes: Fauna Robotics Sprout: 107cm, 29 DoF. Acquired by Amazon Mar 2026.

f117.investigation: Raion Robotics makes quadruped 'Raibo' (KAIST spinoff). No humanoid 'LION' product found.

f120.notes: Pudu PUDU D7: semi-humanoid wheeled robot, 165cm, 30 DoF (50 w/ dexterous hand).

f125.notes: Sulu.be Steve: 210cm artistic humanoid (theater character, not commercial).

f126.investigation: CasiVision is AOI/machine-vision company, NOT humanoid maker. Likely confused with CASBOT 01 by 中科慧灵.

f127.notes: Pudu PUDU D9: 170cm, 42 DoF, 352 N·m peak torque.

f129.notes: DOBOT Atom Max: 41 DoF, 1500 TOPS AI module.

f131.notes: Keenon XMAN-R1 (Mar 2025): embodied service humanoid for hospitality/logistics.

f133.notes: LG CLOiD — humanoid home robot. Announced Dec 25 2025.

f134.notes: Holiday Robotics (Korea) Friday: 176cm, 63 DoF, wheeled base 1.9 m/s.

f137.notes: TOPSTAR Xiao Tuo — duplicate of f97.

f138.notes: PHYBOT C1: 128cm compact home humanoid with HD projection.

f141.notes: PL-Universe ProWhite 2.0 wheeled humanoid + PL-WitHand. JD.com partnership.

f142.notes: SwitchBot Onero H1: ~1.3m, 22 DoF arms, ~$1,500. NOTE: input typo 'OMERO H1' → 'Onero H1'; company should be 'SwitchBot/Wonderlabs', NOT Ecovacs.

f145.notes: GigaAI (Huawei-backed) Maker H01: wheeled humanoid, 28 DoF. 'Physical AGI'.

f146.notes: AIDOL Lab (Russia) AIDOL: Stage debut Nov 2025 ended in fall.
